title: Stuffed peppers
intro: Stuffed peppers appear across Central and Eastern Europe, the Balkans, the Mediterranean, and the American Midwest. Hollowed peppers are filled with rice and aromatics, then cooked in a tomato-based liquid until the flavors come together.

ingredientGroups.title: Pepper preparation
items: 4 large bell peppers, tops cut off and seeds removed

ingredientGroups.title: Braising liquid
items: 2 cups Low Sodium V8 vegetable juice

ingredientGroups.title: Finishing sauce
items: ½ small onion, very finely minced
2 cloves garlic, minced to a paste

stepGroups.title: Instructions
items:
Prepare the peppers
Bring salted water to a boil. Add peppers and cook 4–5 minutes until just beginning to soften. Drain upside down. Lightly salt inside and coat outside with olive oil.

Cook the rice
Bring salted water to a boil. Add rice and cook 7 minutes. Drain immediately and spread to steam dry.

Build the filling base
Heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onion and cook until soft and lightly golden, 8–10 minutes.
Add tomato paste and cook until darkened and slightly sticky.
Add garlic and cook briefly.
Add crumbles over medium-high heat. Let them sit, then cook until lightly browned and moisture is driven off. Add stock, soy sauce, and Worcestershire. Cook until absorbed and cohesive.

Finish the filling
Reduce heat to low. Add rice, herbs, spices, salt, and pepper. Mix thoroughly.
Add red wine vinegar and fold in.
The filling should hold together when pressed but remain light. Adjust slightly with stock or heat if needed.
Rest 2–3 minutes.

Fill the peppers
Place peppers upright in a snug vessel. Fill fully, pressing lightly to remove air gaps. Leave ¼ inch at top. Add jalapeño slices.

Add braising liquid
Pour liquid to reach roughly halfway up the peppers.

Braise the peppers
Cover and bake at 375°F for 20 minutes.
Check for doneness: a knife should pass easily through the pepper wall while it still holds shape.
Uncover and bake 6–8 minutes to lightly set the tops.
Remove and rest 5 minutes.

Build the finishing sauce
Heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onion and cook slowly until fully softened.
Add tomato paste and cook until darkened.
Add garlic and cook gently.
Add roasted red pepper and fully break down into the base.
Add stock and ½ cup braising liquid. Simmer and reduce until cohesive and lightly thickened.Add paprika and simmer briefly.

Finish the sauce
Add red wine vinegar and lemon juice. Adjust salt.

Serving
Spoon sauce onto the plate and place peppers on top. Finish with parsley.
